key_insight: DFT predicts that lead-free double perovskites K/Rb2SnGeX6 (X = Cl, Br, I) are stable, ductile, and highly tunable, with bandgaps shrinking from 1.44 to 0.64 eV and thermoelectric performance peaking at ZT = 2.4 for K2SnGeI6 at 1000 K.
engineering_relevance: This family is a promising low-toxicity materials platform for next-generation photovoltaics, near-infrared photodetectors, tandem solar cells, and waste-heat recovery devices.
method: First-principles DFT calculations on structure, elasticity, electronic bands, optics, and thermoelectrics
Stability screening using formation energy, tolerance factor, and octahedral factor
Transport assessment from band structure, carrier effective mass, lattice thermal conductivity, and ZT
evidence_signal: The abstract reports all six compounds as thermodynamically stable in a cubic phase, all as ductile, direct-bandgap semiconductors, and identifies K2SnGeI6 as the top thermoelectric candidate with ZT = 2.4 at 1000 K.
watch_item: The results are computational and may require experimental validation of phase stability, defect tolerance, and real-device performance under synthesis and operating conditions.
paper_title: First-Principles Study of Novel Lead-Free Double Perovskite \b{eta}2SnGeX6 (\b{eta} = K, Rb; X = Cl, Br, I) for thermomechanical, optoelectronic and outstanding thermoelectric applications
